data.title: Monro appoints Peter Fitzsimmons as CEO
data.description:
• Monro (MNRO) announced on Wednesday that it has entered into a formal employment agreement directly with Peter Fitzsimmons to continue as the company’s full-time president and chief executive officer, effective immediately.
  • The company also said that Fitzsimmons will also join the company’s board of directors and executive committee, effective immediately.
  • Fitzsimmons previously served as the president and chief executive officer of the company pursuant to an engagement letter between the company and AP Services, an affiliate of AlixPartners. 
  • In connection with this announcement, Fitzsimmons has resigned from AlixPartner and its affiliate AP Services, LLC, effective December 2, 2025.
  • MNRO +0.10% premarket to $18.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Enphase starts shipments of IQ EV Charger 2 in U.S.
data.description:
Enphase Energy (ENPH) +0.8% pre-market Wednesday after announcing the first production shipments of its next-generation IQ EV Charher 2 to customers across the U.S.

The IQ EV Charger 2 "uses solar-aware charging that captures surplus solar power and adjusts charging in 1-amp steps to maximize clean energy use," the company said, adding that a single SKU supporting up to a 60-amp breaker simplifies inventory for installers, RFID access control keeps charging secure, and load balancing helps avoid panel upgrades by matching power to available capacity.

"EV charging is becoming a natural extension of the home energy system, and the IQ EV Charger 2 gives homeowners a smarter, faster, and more beautiful way to charge with solar," Enphase (ENPH) Senior VP Jayant Somani said.

In addition to the first U.S. shipments, the IQ EV Charger 2 is now also available in Canada, following its debut earlier this year across Europe, Australia, and New Zealand, with additional global availability planned for 2026, the company said.

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange’s November average daily volume up 5% Y/Y
data.description:
• Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), which operates the New York Stock Exchange, reported 5% Y/Y growth in total average daily volume ((ADV)) for November.
  • Open interest rose 14% Y/Y, including a record interest of 112.9M lots on November 21.
  • Total Energy ADV grew 7% Y/Y, with oil ADV up 12% Y/Y. Total Financials ADV, meanwhile, rose 4% Y/Y, with open interest up 33% Y/Y, including record futures of 15.7M lots on November 21.

data.title: DiamondRock Hospitality declares $0.08 dividend
data.description:
• DiamondRock Hospitality (DRH) declares $0.08/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • The company also declared stub dividend of $0.04/share, bringing total dividend to $0.36/share in 2025, up 12.5% from prior dividend of $0.32 in 2024.
  • Forward yield 3.59%
  • Payable Jan. 14; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See DRH Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Box tumbles after mixed Q3, analysts remain largely neutral
data.description:
Shares of Box (BOX) fell about 8% premarket on Wednesday after third quarter results, while analysts held largely neutral views.

RBC Capital kept its Underperform rating and $26 price target on the shares.

"Box delivered an in-line quarter, sustaining healthy execution across billings, margins, and FCF while maintaining disciplined expense control," said analysts led by Rishi Jaluria.

The analysts noted that revenue grew 9% year-over-year (8% cc), slightly above consensus, supported by continued Enterprise Advanced adoption and Al-driven upgrades. While growth remains steady rather than accelerating, improving net revenue retention, or NRR (104%), and strong remaining performance obligations, or RPO, trends underscore durable customer engagement, according to the analysts.

"With management reaffirming FY26 revenue guidance of ~$1.175B (+8% YoY) and introducing a $150M buyback expansion, we view results as balanced, but unlikely to alter sentiment near term," said Jaluria and his team.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Equal-weight rating and $38 price target on Box's stock.

Analysts led by Josh Baer said that Box reported a "strong" set of third quarter results, with a top-line beat and raise, bullish leading indicators, and strong commentary around AI and Suites. Billings grew +12% year-over-year in constant currency, outperforming guidance for +8% year-over-year growth in constant currency.

"Double-digit growth across all leading indicators (RPO/Bookings/ Billings) suggests it is only a matter of time before revenue growth follows. Until then, investors can get a taste through an improving NRR, driven not only by pricing uplift from Enterprise Advanced, but also seat growth," said Baer and his team.

BofA reiterated its Buy rating and $40 price target on Box's shares.

The analysts said new AI capabilities in Enterprise Advanced are widening the breadth of potential Box use cases — inspiring a pronounced upgrade cycle and reinvigorating all key elements of Box’s growth algorithm.

"Visibility to 10-15% revenue growth improving Growth levers outlined at Box’s March Analyst Day – including upsells/seat expansions, new customer acquisition, and platform expansion – are all tracking positively relative to initial expectations detailed in the framework to return to 10-15% growth. While a sustained growth reacceleration remains a show-me story, a material positive re-rate is warranted if the company can achieve its goal," said the analysts.

Related stock: Shares of Dropbox (DBX) were largely flat premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: American Resources secures $5M credit facility with Old National Bank
data.description:
• American Resources (AREC) announced on Wednesday it has secured a $5 million inventory line of credit with Old National Bank.
  • The credit facility enables American Resources to finance the procurement of end-of-life material, manufacturing scrap, ores and concentrated to be processed into high-value mineral feedstock. 
  • "This collaboration with Old National Bank provides us with the liquidity and flexibility to accelerate our feedstock acquisition strategy and expand the material entering ReElement Technologies' refining platform," said Kirk Taylor, Chief Financial Officer of American Resources Corporation. 
  • AREC +1.04% premarket to $2.89.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ventripoint Diagnostics appoints David Swetlow as CFO
data.description:
• Ventripoint Diagnostics (VPT:CA) announced on Wednesday the appointment of David Swetlow as chief financial officer ((CFO)), effective immediately. 
  • The board of directors has awarded Swetlow one million options exercisable at $0.11 with a term of 10 years and vesting over 12 months.
  • As CFO, Swetlow will oversee financial strategy, capital markets, financial reporting and operations, working closely with the executive team and Board of Directors as VPT advances commercialization efforts in the U.S., Canada, Europe, the UK, and other priority markets, the company said. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities trades lower as 2026 FFO guidance falls short of consensus
data.description:
Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) was trading lower as the life sciences REIT delivered 2026 FFO guidance below consensus.

Shares were -5.20% Wednesday pre-market to $51.03.

The company expects 2026 FFO per share to be in the range of $6.25 to $6.55, or $6.40 at the midpoint.

Comparatively, the consensus estimate stands at $6.91.

The company estimates Q4 2026 FFO per share to be in the range of $1.40 to $1.60, down from an expected Q4 2025 FFO per share range of between $2.13 and $2.19. Q4 2026 FFO consensus stands at $1.64.

Detailed guidance can be found here.

data.title: Lakeland secures order from Malaysia fire and rescue department
data.description:
• Lakeland Industries (LAKE) on Wednesday said it has received an order from the Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia for firefighter personal protective equipment.
  • Terms of the order were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hawkins acquires Redbird Chemical to expand Texas footprint
data.description:
• Hawkins (HWKN) said on Wednesday it has acquired the assets of Redbird Chemical, a distributor serving water treatment and industrial customers in eastern Texas.
  • “Redbird has built a strong business that is well-connected with the local communities, and we intend to maintain those connections." Chief Executive Patrick Hawkins said in a statement.
  • HWKN -0.0% premarket to $134.78.
  • Source: Press Release
